flashcards.category: AP Physics C: E&M - Electric Circuits

cards.back: $I = \frac{dq}{dt}$. Rate of charge flow. SI unit: Ampere (A) = C/s.
Direction of positive charge flow (opposite to electron flow).
$\vec{J} = nq\vec{v}_d$, where $n$ is charge carrier density, $v_d$ is drift velocity.
Average velocity of charge carriers in an electric field, typically $\sim 10^{-4}\ \text{m/s}$.
Intrinsic property of material. $\rho = \frac{E}{J}$. SI unit: $\Omega\cdot\text{m}$.

cards.front: What is the same for resistors in series?
cards.back: Current $I$ is the same through each resistor.

cards.front: What is the same for resistors in parallel?
cards.back: Potential difference $V$ is the same across each resistor.

cards.front: Two resistors in parallel: short formula?

cards.back: Work per unit charge done by a source (battery) to drive charge through a circuit. SI unit: V.

cards.front: Power dissipated in a resistor? (3 forms)

cards.back: $\sum I_{\text{in}} = \sum I_{\text{out}}$. Conservation of charge at a node.
$\sum_{loop} \Delta V = 0$. Conservation of energy around a closed loop.

cards.front: Sign convention: traversing a resistor in direction of current?
Sign convention: traversing a battery from $-$ to $+$?

cards.back: Two resistors in series: $V_{\text{out}} = V_{\text{in}}\frac{R_2}{R_1 + R_2}$.
Two resistors in parallel: $I_2 = I_{\text{in}}\frac{R_1}{R_1 + R_2}$.
Circuit for measuring unknown resistance. Balanced when $R_1/R_2 = R_3/R_x$, no current through galvanometer.

cards.front: What is an **ammeter** and how is it connected?
cards.back: Measures current. Connected in **series**. Has very low internal resistance.

cards.front: What is a **voltmeter** and how is it connected?
cards.back: Measures voltage. Connected in **parallel**. Has very high internal resistance.
Circuit containing a resistor and capacitor. Charging/discharging governed by $\tau = RC$.
$\tau = RC$. Time for charge to reach $1 - 1/e \approx 63\%$ of final value.
